notes: Phase 01 is the DD-GRAPH-13 fail-for-right-reason gate: lands the pure crates/scoring crate (ADTs + score/weight_bucket/ScoringConfig::DEFAULT stubs), the four StoragePort read methods + ADTs in ports, the adapter-duckdb recursive-CTE + scoring-feed read-method stubs, the cli 6-flag dispatch (todo!() bodies), the xtask anti-merging-extension + scoring pure-core allowlist, and the test-support seeder + fixtures_scoring + assertion helpers + the two [[test]] registrations (DD-GRAPH-14). After Phase 01 all 33 slice-04 ATs classify RED (panic at todo!()), not BROKEN.

Phase 02 ships the layer-2 pure-core scoring scenarios (scoring_core.rs SC-1..SC-6) FIRST: the 4 @property formula invariants (reproducibility SC-1/Gate 2, determinism SC-2, sparse-stays-sparse SC-3/Gate 3, triangulation-monotonicity SC-4) + the 2 example-pinned (anti-merging type contract SC-5/Gate 1, cross-project-triangulation-counts-as-breadth SC-6/Q-DELIVER-SCORE-1). These drive the pure scoring crate fully and unblock the layer-3 weighted/explain scenarios in Phase 05 (the renderers only need to verify the user-visible rendering of already-proven invariants).

Phase 03 (query by dimension) ships the GQE-1..9 object/contributor scenarios (US-GRAPH-001/002 + J-002a) + the recursive-CTE UNION-ALL dimension/scoring-feed adapter impls. GQE-1 is the slice-04 walking-skeleton (object dimension); GQE-2/GQE-9 carry the anti-merging row-shape; GQE-5 is the local-first guardrail.

Phase 04 (traverse) ships the GQE-20..25 traversal scenarios (US-GRAPH-004 + J-002b) + the cycle-safe depth-bounded recursive CTE. GQE-20 is a walking-skeleton + the KPI-GRAPH-1 north-star connection; GQE-24 is Gate 5 (every edge maps to a signed claim).

Phase 05 (weighted + explain + sparse + anti-merging-in-aggregates) ships GQE-10..19 + GQE-26 + GQE-27 (US-GRAPH-003/005/006 + J-002c). The load-bearing release-gates land here: GQE-11 (Gate 3 sparse_renders_sparse), GQE-14 (Gate 4 weight_and_bucket_never_persisted), GQE-16 (Gate 1 + Gate 2 explain reproduces), GQE-26 (Gate 6 scoring_uses_numeric_confidence), GQE-27 (e2e Gate 1 + Gate 4 wiring).

Anti-merging-in-aggregates (WD-73/WD-88/I-GRAPH-2; extends slice-03 I-FED-1) is three-layer: TYPE (non-Option author_did on AttributedClaim/Contribution/GraphEdge + non-empty WeightedPairing.contributions — Phase 01 lands the types, SC-5 proves it at layer 2); STRUCTURAL (xtask no_cross_table_join_elides_author extended to scoring-feed + traversal SQL — Phase 01 + Phase 03/04); BEHAVIORAL (scoring_aggregate_preserves_attribution — GQE-1/16/26 + SC-5 in Phase 02/03/05). Aggregation happens in PURE Rust (scoring::score), NEVER in SQL (no SUM/GROUP BY across authors); the scoring-feed query returns per-claim attributed rows.

Weights/buckets are DERIVED + display-only + NEVER persisted (WD-72/WD-89/I-GRAPH-3): no persistence code path; the no-persist test scans every DuckDB table + on-disk artifact for adherence_weight / STRONG / MODERATE / SPARSE substrings (Gate 4; GQE-14 + GQE-27 in Phase 05).

Scoring is transparent/auditable, no ML (WD-71/WD-86/I-GRAPH-1): ScoringConfig::DEFAULT is a compile-time const SSOT (Q-DELIVER-4; author_distinct_bonus=0.25, cross_project_triangulation_bonus=0.5); the formula is printed in --weighted output; --explain reproduces the per-claim running sum by hand (Gate 2; SC-1 + GQE-16). DELIVER may tune the constants (a code + test change), never a config file or a learned weight.

Read-only / local-first (WD-79/WD-92/I-GRAPH-7): NO slice-04 scenario creates or signs a claim — claims are SEEDED as preconditions via the slice-03 PeerPds + claim add / peer add / peer pull seam (DD-GRAPH-2); scoring/traversal/dimension queries READ them. Bounded traversal default depth 2 (WD-76/WD-91), cycle-safe via a visited-set guard. Every explorer verb succeeds with the network disabled (GQE-5/15/25 + run_openlore_network_disabled).

Q-DELIVER resolutions baked into implementation_notes: Q-DELIVER-1 (exact recursive-CTE SQL = the data-models.md delimited visited-path string + depth column) at 01-03 + Phase 04; Q-DELIVER-2 (one query_attributed_for_scoring(ScoringFilter) filtered method, NOT three thin methods) at 01-02 + 01-03; Q-DELIVER-3 (scoring module split = formula.rs + bucket.rs + types.rs for mutation-test clarity) at 01-01; Q-DELIVER-4 (ScoringConfig compile-time const SSOT) at 01-01 + Phase 02; Q-DELIVER-5 (STRONG/MODERATE/SPARSE thresholds = WD-77/ADR-022 defaults; DISTILL asserts only the NOT-Sparse half for triangulated cargo) at 02-06 + Phase 05; Q-DELIVER-6 (Connections-found callout + omitted-edge + sparse-honesty line formats match the journey/user-story mockup; the named assertion helpers pin the content contract) at Phase 04 + Phase 05; Q-DELIVER-7 (explorer orientation message is optional, NOT load-bearing — no slice-04 AT requires it) deferred, recorded at 01-04.

SCORE-1 / WD-90 bucket rule (the resolved # DISTILL: confirm flag): cross-project triangulation by the SAME author counts toward evidence breadth for the bucket — a triangulated single-claim pairing (cargo, Rachel spanning cargo+nixpkgs) is NOT Sparse; a single claim with NO triangulation AND NO co-author STAYS Sparse regardless of confidence magnitude (tokio at 0.50; a 0.99 single claim too). Pinned by SC-3 (sparse leg) + SC-6 (triangulation leg) at layer 2 and GQE-11 (sparse) + GQE-19 (cargo triangulation explained) at layer 3.

Mutation testing scope (D-D31, the SECOND widening after slice-02 D-D23): crates/scoring (formula.rs + bucket.rs) joins the nightly cargo-mutants --package list at >=95% kill rate (release-tag blocking, PR advisory). adapter-duckdb is NOT mutated (effect shell; covered by the extended probe). The pure-core layer-1 unit/mutation tests in crates/scoring/src/#[cfg(test)] are DELIVER's inner TDD loop, OUT of DISTILL scope (DD-GRAPH-7).

DEVOPS gate jobs (D-D30, NOT a DELIVER roadmap deliverable but the CI mapping): three release-blocking GUARDRAIL ATs (at-scoring-aggregate-preserves-attribution / Gate 1 / GQE-16, at-weight-equals-formula / Gate 2 / GQE-16+SC-1, at-sparse-renders-sparse / Gate 3 / GQE-11+SC-3) + three blocking-on-PR gate ATs (Gate 4/5/6) + four explorer-scenario ATs. DELIVER also lands the extended adapter-duckdb probe + the scripts/kpi-graph-*.jq snippets + the graph.* tracing events per the DEVOPS handoff.

DD-GRAPH-10 state-delta migration: load-bearing scenarios (GQE-14 never-persisted, GQE-16 explain-sums, GQE-11 sparse, GQE-20 traversal, GQE-1/GQE-26 attribution) carry an implementation_notes call-out to migrate from named assertion helpers (assert_weight_not_persisted, assert_explain_sums_to_weight, assert_sparse_rendered_as_sparse, assert_every_edge_has_backing_claim, assert_weight_decomposes_to_per_author) to assert_state_delta(before, after, universe, expected) over PORT-EXPOSED universe names (cli.graph_query.bucket[subject], cli.graph_query.explain.running_sum[subject], storage.duckdb.no_weight_or_bucket_column, cli.graph_query.traverse.edge_cids) once the helper bodies are real. NEVER internal scoring/StoragePort struct fields.
